description_long: This research institute is a large, modern building complex featuring two main interconnected structures: a taller tower and a lower, elongated wing. The facade is predominantly white with horizontal blue-tinted window bands, giving it a sleek and contemporary appearance. The building sits on a landscaped plot with green areas and a surrounding driveway or access road, suggesting vehicular and pedestrian access. The tower section has a series of vertical window slits on one side, while the lower wing features larger horizontal windows, likely for communal or laboratory spaces. The overall design emphasizes clean lines, geometric forms, and functional layout, typical of institutional architecture. No visible signage or decorative elements are present, reinforcing a minimalist, utilitarian aesthetic.
description: A large, modern research institute with a white facade, blue-tinted windows, and two interconnected structures set on landscaped grounds.
description_view_0: The main facade shows both the tall tower and the elongated wing, with prominent horizontal blue windows and landscaped surroundings.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the narrow profile of the tower and the vertical window slits, with the driveway curving around the building.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid, windowless back of the tower and the full length of the lower wing, with minimal architectural detailing.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the junction between the tower and the wing, with visible blue window bands and the access road encircling the building.
attribute_entryway_design: The main entryway is not distinctly visible, but appears to be integrated into the lower wing, likely featuring wide glass doors for accessibility. The entrance is flush with the facade, with no visible steps, suggesting level access suitable for wheelchairs. There are no decorative elements or prominent canopies, maintaining a minimalist, functional design.
